title: ferray-core: strides are element-counted, not byte-counted — limits dtype-erased operations
description:
Question: 

Context: 

Findings: 

NumPy strides are in bytes, allowing dtype-erased pointer arithmetic on char* buffers. ferray-core strides are in elements (inherited from ndarray). This works fine for typed operations but makes it impossible to write dtype-generic iteration over raw buffers without knowing T at compile time. DynArray partially addresses this but each operation requires a match over all variants. Consider whether a byte-stride layer could be added beneath the typed layer for operations like copyto, ascontiguousarray, and buffer protocol interop.
